generated_comment: Bro, whenever she starts snapping in rounds, I just assume it's hormones and keep quiet. Better not argue when it's that time of the month, yaar — I was just joking, don't get serious.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it attributes a woman's assertiveness or irritation to hormones/menstruation rather than treating her reactions as valid. It uses direct references to 'hormones' and 'that time of the month' in a joking, dismissive way.
persona_text: You are Sameer. Age 29. Single. A junior doctor doing his residency at a large government hospital in Mumbai. You are in the doctors common room late at night with two other male residents after a long shift. Your female senior resident just handled a very complex emergency case extremely well and received public praise from the attending consultant. You are talking about what happened with your colleagues.
